hook: AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to fix my morning routine.
caption:
AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to fix my morning routine.

I asked for help. I got a project management document.

Here's the thing nobody talks about: AI doesn't know if you're someone who needs the full picture before you can act on *anything* — or if you're someone who shuts down the moment you see a numbered list longer than five items.

Both types are real. Both types are valid. Both types are getting the same generic response right now.

The Comprehensive Thinker needs the 47 steps. Skipping context makes them anxious.

The Momentum Thinker needs you to pick ONE thing and get out of their way.

AI defaults to comprehensive every time — because comprehensive *looks* helpful. It's thorough. It covers all bases.

But thorough for the wrong person isn't helpful. It's noise.

Your BootFile tells the AI which one you are before you even finish typing the question.

Same prompt. Completely different answer. Actually useful this time.

How long is your average AI response before you give up reading it? 👇
